name: From San Nicolas del Puerto to Cazalla de la Sierra via Guadalcanal - circular through the Sierra Morena
name_original: De San Nicolás del Puerto a Cazalla de la Sierra por Guadalcanal – circular por la Sierra Morena
description: This difficult 62-mile road cycling route through Sierra Morena offers challenging climbs, stunning Mediterranean forests, and unique geolog
region_intro: Road cycling around San Nicolás Del Puerto offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra Morena Natural Park and the Sierra Norte de Sevilla. The region features mountainous terrain, riverbanks, and unique geological formations like the Cerro del Hierro karst landscape. Road cycling routes here traverse varied elevations, providing options for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around San Nicolás Del Puerto The most popular road cycling route is From Constantina to San Nicolás del Puerto - circular through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, a 45.4 miles (73.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers sustained climbs and descents through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From San Nicolas del Puerto to Cazalla de la Sierra via Guadalcanal - circular through the Sierra Morena, a difficult 61.9 miles (99.7 km) path. This route navigates the Sierra Morena, featuring significant elevation changes and scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Sierra Morena de Sevilla Park Loop, a 75.1 miles (120.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra Morena de Sevilla Natural Park, often completed in about 6 hours 17 minutes. Road cycling around San Nicolás Del Puerto is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and the unique karst formations of Cerro del Hierro. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in San Nicolás Del Puerto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore San Nicolás Del Puerto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around San Nicolás Del Puerto?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes available around San Nicolás Del Puerto, offering a variety of distances and elevation gains to suit different preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in San Nicolás Del Puerto?
A: The region is characterized by diverse terrain, including mountainous areas, lush riverbanks, and unique geological formations like the karst landscape of Cerro del Hierro. You'll encounter sustained climbs and descents, particularly within the Sierra Morena Natural Park and the Sierra Norte de Sevilla.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in San Nicolás Del Puerto?
A: San Nicolás Del Puerto is ideal for road cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be quite hot, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler, wetter periods. Checking local weather forecasts before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, the area offers 12 difficult road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993987296/sierra-morena-de-sevilla-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena de Sevilla Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 75.1-mile (120.9 km) trail with significant elevation changes, often taking over 6 hours to complete.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994018505/from-constantina-to-san-nicolas-del-puerto-circular-through-the-sierra-norte-de-sevilla-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Constantina to San Nicolás del Puerto - circular through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a 45.4-mile (73.1 km) route through the natural park.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes?
A: You can explore several natural monuments and scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning views of the unique karst landscape, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; provide a picturesque setting along the Huéznar River.

Q: Is there a greenway suitable for cycling in San Nicolás Del Puerto?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Norte de Sevilla Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a fantastic option. This 19-kilometer former railway line is mostly flat, well-marked, and shaded, making it accessible for all cycling levels. It runs between Cazalla-Constantina and San Nicolás del Puerto, passing through tunnels and old mining sites.

Q: Are there any historical sites to visit while road cycling?
A: Near the artificial beach in San Nicolás del Puerto, you can find an ancient Roman bridge over the River Galindón. Although reformed in medieval times, it adds a historical charm to your cycling experience and is restricted to pedestrians and bicycles.

Q: What are the options for bike rental in San Nicolás Del Puerto?
A: Bike rental services, including electric bikes, are available directly in San Nicolás del Puerto, making it convenient for visitors who prefer not to bring their own equipment.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in San Nicolás Del Puerto?
A: The road cycling routes in San Nicolás Del Puerto are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain and the scenic beauty of the Sierra Morena and Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Parks.

Q: Are there any routes that connect San Nicolás del Puerto to other towns?
A: Yes, you can find routes connecting to nearby towns. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995620065/from-san-nicolas-del-puerto-to-cazalla-de-la-sierra-via-guadalcanal-circular-through-the-sierra-morena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From San Nicolas del Puerto to Cazalla de la Sierra via Guadalcanal - circular through the Sierra Morena&lt;/a&gt; is a 61.9-mile (99.7 km) path that navigates through the Sierra Morena, offering significant elevation changes and scenic views between towns.
tips: It is a Gothic-Mudejar temple, built in the place where there was previously a primitive Muslim fortress, from the Almohad period.
Cazalla de la Sierra is a beautiful town with a lot of history. Worth a visit.

The river beach is the only area in San Nicolás where bathing is allowed. The Galindón riverbed is regulated by a system of gates and dams that make bathing safe for everyone. It has access stairs, toilets and picnic areas. It is open from June 15 to September 15 and admission is free.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular route is approximately 99.7 kilometers long and typically takes around 4 hours and 40 minutes to complete by racebike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a 'Hard road ride' with significant elevation gain. While primarily intended for road cycling, be aware that some segments may be unpaved or in poor condition, making them difficult to ride. The route features winding roads that conform to the mountainous relief of the Sierra Morena, traversing diverse landscapes of Mediterranean forest.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness' due to its considerable length (nearly 100 km) and substantial elevation gain (over 1,300 meters). It is best suited for experienced road cyclists looking for a significant challenge.

faqs.question: What natural features and scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You will cycle through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, part of the Sierra Morena Biosphere Reserve. Expect rich Mediterranean forests with holm oak, cork oak, and chestnut trees, numerous rivers, and views of hills and reservoirs. The area is known for its isolated natural environments and potential wildlife sightings.

faqs.question: What villages and landmarks will I pass through on this route?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through charming Andalusian villages such as San Nicolás del Puerto, Cazalla de la Sierra, and Guadalcanal. You'll pass by the Parish of Santa María de la Asunción and the town of Cazalla de la Sierra. The San Nicolás del Puerto Beach is also a notable point near the start/end.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Morena region can get very hot in summer. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, with milder weather and beautiful natural scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in San Nicolás del Puerto, you can typically find parking within the village. Specific parking areas are usually available near the town center or main access points, but it's advisable to check local regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, it is primarily a road cycling route. Bringing dogs on a racebike can be challenging. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can be safely transported or kept on a leash in appropriate areas, especially in villages and near wildlife.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. Notably, it covers about 11% of the Sendero de Sierra Morena and 10% of the Transandalus. Smaller portions also intersect with trails like Alanís Comarcal Norte and Alanís - Cerro del Hierro - San Nicholás del Puerto.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or water along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several villages like San Nicolás del Puerto, Cazalla de la Sierra, and Guadalcanal, you will find opportunities to refuel. These villages typically offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can purchase food and water.

faqs.question: What is an insider tip for tackling this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and potential for unpaved sections, consider using a road bike with slightly wider tires (if possible) or a gravel bike for added comfort and stability. This will help you better navigate any rougher patches without compromising too much on speed on the paved sections. Also, pack sufficient water and snacks, as some stretches between villages can be quite isolated.

waypoints.name: Parish of Santa María de la Asunción
